title: First boot into Linux
description: Installed Ubuntu 18.04 alongside Windows to fix a laptop that kept overheating on stock drivers. Broke the bootloader twice, learned GRUB the hard way, never went back.

title: Started a CS degree, fell into networking
description: A Cisco-flavored networking module turned into a hobby. Built a home lab with second-hand switches to practice VLANs, DHCP scopes and static routing outside of class.

title: First production web app
description: Shipped a small React + Express inventory tool for a family business. Learned more about deployment, environment variables and log rotation than the React part itself.

title: Went mobile with Flutter
description: Rebuilt a web dashboard as a cross-platform Flutter app so field technicians could use it offline. First real exposure to state management at app scale.

title: Containers everywhere
description: Migrated a fleet of manually configured services to Docker Compose, then to a small self-hosted Kubernetes cluster, mostly to see if I could keep it alive.

title: Security-first mindset
description: Started treating hardening as a first step, not an afterthought: firewall rules before features, least privilege by default, and regular reads of CVE advisories for the stack I run. I also practice penetration testing through labs, document how I approach them, and keep some lab challenges on GitHub without full solutions, including Nyx and Vulnyx.

title: Full-stack, full-stack
description: Building interfaces in React and Flutter, the infrastructure they run on, and the networks that connect them — and writing about it in the Linux Lab section of this site.
